delta.analysis: New skill achieves 100% pass rate (vs 87.5% old). The improvement is in context passing — old skill uses placeholders instead of demonstrating actual context extraction. Trade-off: new skill outputs are ~25% more tokens and ~55% slower due to more detailed context composition.

analyst_observations: Context passing is the key differentiator: new skill shows actual extracted context in agent prompts, old skill uses '[OUTPUT WILL BE INSERTED HERE]' placeholders
Both skills achieve correct workflow selection (100% on that assertion across all tests)
Both skills correctly identify parallel steps — this is well-defined in both versions
The 'subagent_type' parameter is only present in new skill outputs — old skill just uses 'model'
New skill includes Step 0 (Read Project Context) which leads to explicit CLAUDE.md status in every prompt
Token/time increase is expected — more detailed prompts naturally produce more output
The failed assertions in old skill are both about context passing quality, not workflow correctness
